name: Circular Microstrip Patch
description: A circular microstrip patch antenna consists of a circular conducting patch printed on a grounded dielectric substrate. It radiates primarily in the broadside direction with moderate gain and is widely used in wireless communication systems due to its low profile, light weight, and ease of fabrication.

radius.name: Patch Radius
radius.description: Physical radius of the circular patch conductor

substrate_thickness.name: Substrate Thickness
substrate_thickness.description: Thickness of the dielectric substrate between patch and ground plane

dielectric_constant.name: Relative Permittivity
dielectric_constant.description: Relative dielectric constant of the substrate material

loss_tangent.name: Loss Tangent
loss_tangent.description: Dielectric loss tangent of the substrate material

feed_position.name: Feed Position
feed_position.description: Radial distance from center to coaxial feed point for impedance matching

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves calculating the patch radius for resonance, determining substrate parameters, positioning the feed for impedance matching, and optimizing for desired bandwidth and radiation characteristics.

steps.title: Calculate Effective Radius
steps.description: Determine the effective radius accounting for fringing fields

steps.title: Design Physical Radius
steps.description: Calculate physical patch radius for desired resonant frequency
steps.formula: a = F / (2 * fr * sqrt(εr) * sqrt(μ0 * ε0))

steps.title: Determine Substrate Height
steps.description: Select substrate thickness for desired bandwidth and efficiency

steps.title: Calculate Feed Position
steps.description: Position coaxial feed for 50-ohm input impedance

steps.title: Compute Radiation Conductance
steps.description: Calculate radiation conductance for efficiency analysis

equations.radiationPattern: E(θ,φ) = (j * k0 * h * V0 * ae * J1(k0*ae*sin(θ))) / (2 * r * sin(θ))

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition
